target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend ŌĆö stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Motivation (dated, citable).** On **May 13, 2026** Anthropic announced that, starting **June 15, 2026**, Claude subscriptions split into two pools: **interactive usage stays on existing subscription limits** (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai), while **programmatic usage moves onto a separate fixed monthly credit pool** ŌĆö explicitly enumerated as Agent SDK, `claude -p` (the non-interactive command), Claude Code GitHub Actions, **and third-party apps that auth with the subscription through the Agent SDK** (i.e. OpenClaw on the existing `claude-cli` backend). The programmatic credit is fixed per tier (Pro $20 / Max 5x $100 / Max 20x $200 / Team $100/seat / Enterprise $200/seat), billed at API rates, and does **not** roll over. Coverage: [VentureBeat ŌĆö "Anthropic reinstates OpenClaw and third-party agent usage on Claude subscriptions ŌĆö with a catch"](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch), [SiliconANGLE ŌĆö "Anthropic announces ŌĆśprogrammatic credit poolŌĆÖ as agentic tool use rises"](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/14/anthropic-announces-programmatic-credit-pool-agentic-tool-use-rises/).
- **Why this PR exists.** OpenClaw's existing `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` ŌĆö i.e. exactly the non-interactive command the announcement names as moving to the programmatic credit pool on June 15. After June 15 every OpenClaw turn on a Claude subscription will drain the fixed credit pool at API rates rather than count against the user's interactive subscription limits, and Opus turns in particular will exhaust the credit very quickly. This PR adds a second backend that drives `claude` in interactive mode (no `-p`) so OpenClaw stays on the interactive pool that subscribers are actually paying for.
- **What changed.** A new `claude-cli-interactive` backend runs `claude` without `-p` (interactive mode) under a per-turn `bun` wrapper that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy, taps every API SSE event to reconstruct `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records on its own stdout, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload tricks claude into staying in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** The existing `claude-cli` backend is untouched (its predicate `liveSession === "claude-stdio"` matches it alone now); user `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` overrides flow through to the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with only `-p`-only flags stripped; the system CA store is **not** mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` is scoped to the spawned claude child only); non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) pass through unmodified; tool-use / timestamp / "waiting on tool" injection into the reasoning stream is intentionally **out of scope** here and lands as a separate follow-up to #81550 that rides on the `stream_event` JSONL surface this PR establishes.
- AI-assisted: yes ŌĆö drafted with Claude.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge ŌĆö this PR introduces the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend; the follow-up tool-use injection PR will work on both)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression
- [x] This PR adds a new backend behind an opt-in backend id

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `claude` running under OpenClaw in **interactive (subscription) mode** with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- Real environment tested: Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm install), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  - `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌĆö focused vitest run of the anthropic extension test files.
  - Set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` as the active backend in user config, restart the OpenClaw gateway, send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn, observe the wrapper boot in stderr, the API SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>` and arrive at OpenClaw as `stream_event` records.
  - Send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` (default sessionMode) and verify the second turn picks up the existing claude session.
  - With `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` set, verify stderr includes `[openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: ŌĆ”\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` (the npm-installed claude, not a winget-shadowed install).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌåÆ `Test Files 7 passed (7) / Tests 73 passed (73) / Duration 10.41s`. New `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` covers the three normalizeConfig branches (override path ŌåÆ `command="bun"` + env passthrough; preserves other env entries; **no override ŌåÆ env passthrough omitted** so wrapper.ts can fall back to a PATH lookup of `claude`).
  - Live Telegram turn: per-turn process emits `init` ŌåÆ `stream_event*` ŌåÆ single `result` on `end_turn`, all sourced from the proxy's SSE tap.
- Observed result after fix: per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly ŌĆö wrapper emits `init`, then a continuous stream of `stream_event` records (tapped from the proxy), then a single `result` record on `end_turn`, then sets `intentionalKill = true` and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits with code 0.
- **Live capture from the running install** ŌĆö redacted artifacts on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch of my fork:
  - [`.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt) ŌĆö 30 lines from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` covering three real per-turn invocations: gateway logs `cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive` + `cli argv: bun <NPM>\ŌĆ”\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose ŌĆ”`, wrapper stderr logs `certs ready` ŌåÆ `MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081` ŌåÆ `spawning claude binary: <NPM>\ŌĆ”\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` ŌåÆ `result flushed, waiting for session write before kill` ŌåÆ `session file updated, killing claude`. Verbatim, just paths/session-ids redacted.
  - [`.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt) ŌĆö `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` directory listing + `openssl x509 -noout` output for both certs. CA: `CN=OpenClaw Proxy CA`, self-signed, valid `May 14 22:30:14 2026 GMT` ŌåÆ `May 14 22:30:14 2027 GMT`, SHA1 `63:69:13:08:D9:AE:CC:CC:85:D3:A7:B2:AB:E0:A6:06:E3:88:97:2E`. Leaf: `CN=api.anthropic.com`, issued by the CA, SAN `DNS:api.anthropic.com, DNS:statsig.anthropic.com, DNS:console.anthropic.com`.
  - [`.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json) ŌĆö `agents.defaults.model.primary` is `claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-6`; `agents.defaults.models` registers `claude-cli-interactive` runtimes for opus-4-7/4-6/4-5, sonnet-4-6/4-5, haiku-4-5. Production wiring, not a test fixture.

  Embedded sample (one of the three turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

  ```
  cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus promptChars=N trigger=user useResume=true session=present resumeSession=<SESSION> reuse=reusable historyPrompt=present
  cli argv: bun <NPM>\node_modules\openclaw\dist\extensions\anthropic\interactive-proxy\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --permission-mode bypassPermissions --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <TMP>\openclaw-cli-mcp-<TMP>\mcp.json --model opus <prompt:N chars>
  [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
  [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 -- Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
  [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
  [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill, path: <HOME>\.claude\projects\<WORKSPACE>\<SESSION>.jsonl
  [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
  [openclaw-proxy] claude exited, code:  signal: SIGTERM intentional: true
  ```
- What was not tested:
  - Linux/macOS (developed and exercised on Windows 11 only); the wrapper has no Windows-specific code beyond the binary-resolution guard, but I have not run it on Linux/macOS.
  - Bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only the `mcp__openclaw__*` allowlist path was exercised live).
  - Behaviour against an Anthropic-side `429`/`overloaded_error` mid-stream (the wrapper passes the SSE stream through unchanged, so the existing claude error-handling should apply, but I did not force the failure to confirm).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö this is a feature PR, not a bugfix. The condition this addresses (no thinking visibility on subscription) is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` ŌĆö asserts `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape (forces `command="bun"`, stashes inherited binary on env when an override exists, omits the env when no override exists).
- Scenario the test should lock in: the post-merge normalize step survives both shapes the runtime can produce ŌĆö (a) user has a `claude-cli` override ŌåÆ `merged.command` is the inherited absolute path, env passthrough must be set; (b) user has no override ŌåÆ `merged.command` stays as the backend's own `"bun"`, env passthrough must be **omitted** so wrapper.ts doesn't try to spawn bun as the claude binary.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig`, fully exercised by calling `backend.normalizeConfig(merged)` synchronously and asserting the output keys. An e2e run would also catch it but cost minutes per run; the unit test covers all three branches in milliseconds and runs in CI on every PR.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A ŌĆö the file is new with this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A ŌĆö three new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New backend id: `claude-cli-interactive`. Selectable via `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` user config; nothing changes for users on `claude-cli`.
- When the new backend is active, every claude turn spawns a fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` process that boots a loopback MITM proxy on `127.0.0.1:<port>` for the lifetime of that turn. Process is killed on `end_turn`; loopback ports are released.
- Reasoning stream now carries plaintext `thinking_delta` content on the subscription path (previously visible only on the API-key billing path).
- Title-generation auxiliary turns (claude's side request whose only text content is `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}`) are buffered and silently suppressed before the wrapper emits any records to OpenClaw.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
OpenClaw gateway
  Ōöé  spawns one fresh wrapper per turn:
  Ō¢╝
bun wrapper.ts [--session-id ŌĆ”] [--resume ŌĆ”] [prompt-as-positional-arg]
  Ōö£ŌöĆ cert-manager.ts ŌåÆ generate/cache local CA + api.anthropic.com leaf cert
  Ōö£ŌöĆ mitm-server.ts  ŌåÆ Stage 1: HTTP CONNECT proxy (loopback only)
  Ōöé                    Stage 2: Bun TLS server (terminates TLS, re-issues upstream)
  Ōöé                    onEvent(evt) tap on every parsed SSE event
  Ō¢╝ NODE_OPTIONS="--require <abs>/tty-spoof.cjs"
    HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>
    NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=<ca.crt>   ŌåÉ scoped to this claude child only
  Ō¢╝
claude.exe (interactive mode, no -p, ink-based TUI rendering)
  Ōöé  API SSE ŌåÆ CONNECT ŌåÆ TLS terminate ŌåÆ tap event ŌåÆ forward to claude
  Ōöé  TUI stdout ŌåÆ /dev/null (we ignore it)
  Ō¢╝
wrapper.ts emits init / stream_event* / result JSONL on its own stdout,
sourced entirely from the tapped SSE ŌĆö output identical to the shape
`claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces.
```

### Per-turn kill/restart lifecycle (why we don't use the persistent live-session model)

`claude-cli-interactive` does **not** use the persistent `claude-stdio` live-session model that `claude-cli` uses. Each turn = one fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` invocation:

1. Wrapper boots, lazily generates/loads the local CA, starts the MITM proxy on a loopback port.
2. Wrapper emits `{"type":"init","session_id":"ŌĆ”"}` on its stdout.
3. Wrapper spawns claude over a piped stdin; framework stdin is forwarded to `claude.stdin` so OpenClaw's prompt-as-stdin convention still works.
4. SSE events flow through the proxy ŌåÆ wrapper's `onEvent` handler ŌåÆ wrapper emits `{"type":"stream_event","event":ŌĆ”}` JSONL per event.
5. On `message_stop` with `stop_reason === "end_turn"`, wrapper assembles `{"type":"result","session_id":"ŌĆ”","result":"ŌĆ”","is_error":false,"usage":{ŌĆ”}}`, drains it to stdout, sets `intentionalKill = true`, then `claude.kill()`s the child.
6. Wrapper exits 0; loopback ports released.

Why kill on `end_turn` instead of letting claude self-exit: interactive claude doesn't exit after one turn ŌĆö it's a TUI awaiting further input. The wrapper has to be authoritative about turn boundaries from the SSE side. `intentionalKill` distinguishes this from claude crashing.

### TTY spoof (`interactive-proxy/tty-spoof.cjs`)

Claude refuses interactive (subscription) mode unless stdin/stdout look like a TTY: it falls back to headless mode the moment `process.stdin.isTTY` is undefined or `tty.isatty(0)` returns false. Under OpenClaw it's spawned with piped stdio, so neither is true. `tty-spoof.cjs` is preloaded into the claude child via `NODE_OPTIONS=--require <abs-path>` and patches:

- `process.stdin.isTTY`, `process.stdout.isTTY`, `process.stderr.isTTY` ŌåÆ `true` (property level).
- `tty.isatty(fd)` ŌåÆ returns `true` for fd 0/1/2 (the ink renderer calls this directly via libuv, bypassing the property).
- `process.stdin.setRawMode` ŌåÆ no-op stub (ink calls this; would throw on a pipe).
- `process.stdout.columns`/`rows` and `process.stderr.columns`/`rows` ŌåÆ defaulted to 200├Ś50 so ink layout has dimensions to render against.

Minimum surface needed for claude to enter and stay in interactive mode under piped stdio. Nothing else is patched ŌĆö claude still does its real work, makes real API calls, and emits its real TUI bytes (we discard them).

### Full JSONL reconstruction (backwards compatibility with `claude -p --output-format stream-json`)

OpenClaw's JSONL parser expects exactly the output shape of `claude -p --output-format stream-json`. The wrapper reproduces it record-for-record, sourced entirely from the proxy:

- **`init`** (session_id) ŌĆö emitted on wrapper boot, before claude even spawns.
- **`stream_event`** (one per tapped SSE event) ŌĆö forwarded verbatim from the proxy: `message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta` (`text_delta` / `thinking_delta` / `signature_delta` / `tool_use`), `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`, `ping`. The proxy emits everything; the wrapper emits everything; OpenClaw's JSONL parser sees the same shape it would have on the `-p` path.
- **`result`** (one per turn) ŌĆö synthesised on `end_turn` from accumulated text-delta concatenation + final usage from `message_delta`.

The proxy is a thin pass-through ŌĆö it tees every parsed SSE event to a single `onEvent` handler and otherwise forwards the SSE stream unmodified to claude. No event-specific logic lives in the proxy; the wrapper interprets everything. To avoid wasted CPU on auxiliary turns the proxy only taps SSE responses for requests that contain a non-empty `tools` array (real conversation turns); title-gen and other no-tool requests pass through without inspection.

### Binary resolution

`normalizeConfig` runs after `mergeBackendConfig` shallow-spreads the inherited `claude-cli` override on top of this backend's base config. The merge replaces `command` with the inherited absolute claude binary path. The normalize step:

- Forces `command` back to `"bun"` so the wrapper actually runs as a script (otherwise `claude.exe` would be spawned with `wrapper.ts` as a positional and the proxy would never start).
- Stashes the inherited binary path on `env.OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` so `wrapper.ts` spawns that exact binary instead of doing a PATH lookup. PATH lookups on Windows resolve via PATHEXT to whichever `claude.exe` arrived first ŌĆö a winget install can shadow the npm install OpenClaw configured.
- **Guards the no-override case**: if `merged.command` is still our backend's own `"bun"` (no `claude-cli` override took effect), the env passthrough is **omitted** so `wrapper.ts` falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"` rather than trying to spawn bun as the claude binary.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **Yes** ŌĆö a per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child only via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store is not modified. Local CA + leaf private key are generated lazily on first use and cached under the user's OpenClaw config dir. The CA exists solely to issue a single `api.anthropic.com` leaf cert that the per-turn child trusts via the env var; no other process consumes it.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** ŌĆö the wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token. It is forwarded transparently in the request that the proxy re-issues upstream over real TLS.
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** ŌĆö claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. The proxy CONNECTs to the real upstream and reissues each request unmodified; the request body is read once to detect the `tools` field for the SSE-tap decision and is forwarded byte-for-byte. Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** ŌĆö same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools set (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same `--setting-sources user`, same `--session-id` semantics.
- Data access scope changed? **No** ŌĆö the proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing is exfiltrated, persisted, or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

Risk + mitigation summary for the Yes items: the trust boundary changes for Anthropic traffic of *this child process only*. Mitigation: child-scoped `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, no system CA mutation, CA private key restricted to the user's OpenClaw config dir, no other process configured to trust it. Backend is opt-in by id (`claude-cli-interactive`), not a default. The whole reason for this surface is to keep OpenClaw on the interactive subscription pool after the June 15 split ŌĆö the `-p` (programmatic) path that doesn't intercept TLS remains the default, and users keep that experience by selecting the existing `claude-cli` backend.

## Prior art / alternatives considered

- [`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) takes a different approach to keeping Claude on the interactive subscription pool: it runs `claude` without `-p` and bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely. That works for the raw final text, but **loses the JSONL `stream_event` records that any reasoning stream or tool-use rendering depends on**. The JSONL-reconstruction approach in this PR (re-emitting the same record shape `claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces, sourced from the SSE tap) preserves the existing OpenClaw stream consumer unchanged ŌĆö so the existing reasoning bridge and the tool-use surface in the follow-up PR layer on cleanly without losing fidelity.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** ŌĆö the existing `claude-cli` backend is unchanged. Adding the new backend is opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- Config/env changes? **No** ŌĆö no new required user config. New optional env var `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stderr from the wrapper.
- Migration needed? **No**.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a newly-installed `claude-cli` user with no inherited override could end up spawning `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` blindly stores `merged.command` in the env passthrough.
  - Mitigation: `normalizeConfig` skips the env passthrough when `merged.command === "bun"`; the wrapper falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"`. Regression test in `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` asserts the env var is `undefined` in that branch.
- Risk: the `tty-spoof.cjs` preload is a runtime support file, not a build output, so it could be omitted from packaged installs and break interactive mode at runtime with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.
  - Mitigation: the file is declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets` so the postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper, mitm-server, and cert-manager.
- Risk: a winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install on Windows because `spawn("claude")` resolves via PATHEXT, leading to `unknown option '--effort'` on older claude versions.
  - Mitigation: when an override is present, `wrapper.ts` spawns the exact binary the parent backend resolved (passed via `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY`) instead of falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- Risk: title-generation side requests can land mid-turn and inject `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - Mitigation: the wrapper buffers early `text_delta`s when the accumulated text starts with `{`; if it parses as JSON with a `title` field the entire turn is suppressed before any record is emitted to OpenClaw. Defence-in-depth: the proxy already skips SSE tapping for requests without a `tools` array, so most auxiliary requests never reach the wrapper at all.
- Risk: TLS interception persists a local CA + leaf key under the user's OpenClaw config dir indefinitely; an attacker with read access to that directory could craft a leaf cert trusted by the per-turn child.
  - Mitigation: trust is scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` (no other process opts in), the system trust store is unchanged, and the CA exists solely to issue the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs. Maintainer security review explicitly invited as part of this PR.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend ŌĆö stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Motivation (dated, citable).** On **May 13, 2026** Anthropic announced that, starting **June 15, 2026**, Claude subscriptions split into two pools: **interactive usage stays on existing subscription limits** (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai), while **programmatic usage moves onto a separate fixed monthly credit pool** ŌĆö explicitly enumerated as Agent SDK, `claude -p` (the non-interactive command), Claude Code GitHub Actions, **and third-party apps that auth with the subscription through the Agent SDK** (i.e. OpenClaw on the existing `claude-cli` backend). The programmatic credit is fixed per tier (Pro $20 / Max 5x $100 / Max 20x $200 / Team $100/seat / Enterprise $200/seat), billed at API rates, and does **not** roll over. Coverage: [VentureBeat ŌĆö "Anthropic reinstates OpenClaw and third-party agent usage on Claude subscriptions ŌĆö with a catch"](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch), [SiliconANGLE ŌĆö "Anthropic announces ŌĆśprogrammatic credit poolŌĆÖ as agentic tool use rises"](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/14/anthropic-announces-programmatic-credit-pool-agentic-tool-use-rises/).
- **Why this PR exists.** OpenClaw's existing `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` ŌĆö i.e. exactly the non-interactive command the announcement names as moving to the programmatic credit pool on June 15. After June 15 every OpenClaw turn on a Claude subscription will drain the fixed credit pool at API rates rather than count against the user's interactive subscription limits, and Opus turns in particular will exhaust the credit very quickly. This PR adds a second backend that drives `claude` in interactive mode (no `-p`) so OpenClaw stays on the interactive pool that subscribers are actually paying for.
- **What changed.** A new `claude-cli-interactive` backend runs `claude` without `-p` (interactive mode) under a per-turn `bun` wrapper that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy, taps every API SSE event to reconstruct `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records on its own stdout, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload tricks claude into staying in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** The existing `claude-cli` backend is untouched (its predicate `liveSession === "claude-stdio"` matches it alone now); user `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` overrides flow through to the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with only `-p`-only flags stripped; the system CA store is **not** mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` is scoped to the spawned claude child only); non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) pass through unmodified; tool-use / timestamp / "waiting on tool" injection into the reasoning stream is intentionally **out of scope** here and lands as a separate follow-up to #81550 that rides on the `stream_event` JSONL surface this PR establishes.
- AI-assisted: yes ŌĆö drafted with Claude.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge ŌĆö this PR introduces the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend; the follow-up tool-use injection PR will work on both)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression
- [x] This PR adds a new backend behind an opt-in backend id

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `claude` running under OpenClaw in **interactive (subscription) mode** with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- Real environment tested: Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm install), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  - `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌĆö focused vitest run of the anthropic extension test files.
  - Set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` as the active backend in user config, restart the OpenClaw gateway, send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn, observe the wrapper boot in stderr, the API SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>` and arrive at OpenClaw as `stream_event` records.
  - Send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` (default sessionMode) and verify the second turn picks up the existing claude session.
  - With `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` set, verify stderr includes `[openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: ŌĆ”\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` (the npm-installed claude, not a winget-shadowed install).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌåÆ `Test Files 7 passed (7) / Tests 73 passed (73) / Duration 10.41s`. New `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` covers the three normalizeConfig branches (override path ŌåÆ `command="bun"` + env passthrough; preserves other env entries; **no override ŌåÆ env passthrough omitted** so wrapper.ts can fall back to a PATH lookup of `claude`).
  - Live Telegram turn: per-turn process emits `init` ŌåÆ `stream_event*` ŌåÆ single `result` on `end_turn`, all sourced from the proxy's SSE tap.
- Observed result after fix: per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly ŌĆö wrapper emits `init`, then a continuous stream of `stream_event` records (tapped from the proxy), then a single `result` record on `end_turn`, then sets `intentionalKill = true` and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits with code 0.
- **Live capture from the running install** ŌĆö redacted artifacts on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch of my fork:
  - [`.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt) ŌĆö 30 lines from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` covering three real per-turn invocations: gateway logs `cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive` + `cli argv: bun <NPM>\ŌĆ”\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose ŌĆ”`, wrapper stderr logs `certs ready` ŌåÆ `MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081` ŌåÆ `spawning claude binary: <NPM>\ŌĆ”\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` ŌåÆ `result flushed, waiting for session write before kill` ŌåÆ `session file updated, killing claude`. Verbatim, just paths/session-ids redacted.
  - [`.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt) ŌĆö `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` directory listing + `openssl x509 -noout` output for both certs. CA: `CN=OpenClaw Proxy CA`, self-signed, valid `May 14 22:30:14 2026 GMT` ŌåÆ `May 14 22:30:14 2027 GMT`, SHA1 `63:69:13:08:D9:AE:CC:CC:85:D3:A7:B2:AB:E0:A6:06:E3:88:97:2E`. Leaf: `CN=api.anthropic.com`, issued by the CA, SAN `DNS:api.anthropic.com, DNS:statsig.anthropic.com, DNS:console.anthropic.com`.
  - [`.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json) ŌĆö `agents.defaults.model.primary` is `claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-6`; `agents.defaults.models` registers `claude-cli-interactive` runtimes for opus-4-7/4-6/4-5, sonnet-4-6/4-5, haiku-4-5. Production wiring, not a test fixture.

  Embedded sample (one of the three turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

  ```
  cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus promptChars=N trigger=user useResume=true session=present resumeSession=<SESSION> reuse=reusable historyPrompt=present
  cli argv: bun <NPM>\node_modules\openclaw\dist\extensions\anthropic\interactive-proxy\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --permission-mode bypassPermissions --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <TMP>\openclaw-cli-mcp-<TMP>\mcp.json --model opus <prompt:N chars>
  [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
  [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 -- Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
  [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
  [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill, path: <HOME>\.claude\projects\<WORKSPACE>\<SESSION>.jsonl
  [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
  [openclaw-proxy] claude exited, code:  signal: SIGTERM intentional: true
  ```
- What was not tested:
  - Linux/macOS (developed and exercised on Windows 11 only); the wrapper has no Windows-specific code beyond the binary-resolution guard, but I have not run it on Linux/macOS.
  - Bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only the `mcp__openclaw__*` allowlist path was exercised live).
  - Behaviour against an Anthropic-side `429`/`overloaded_error` mid-stream (the wrapper passes the SSE stream through unchanged, so the existing claude error-handling should apply, but I did not force the failure to confirm).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö this is a feature PR, not a bugfix. The condition this addresses (no thinking visibility on subscription) is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` ŌĆö asserts `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape (forces `command="bun"`, stashes inherited binary on env when an override exists, omits the env when no override exists).
- Scenario the test should lock in: the post-merge normalize step survives both shapes the runtime can produce ŌĆö (a) user has a `claude-cli` override ŌåÆ `merged.command` is the inherited absolute path, env passthrough must be set; (b) user has no override ŌåÆ `merged.command` stays as the backend's own `"bun"`, env passthrough must be **omitted** so wrapper.ts doesn't try to spawn bun as the claude binary.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig`, fully exercised by calling `backend.normalizeConfig(merged)` synchronously and asserting the output keys. An e2e run would also catch it but cost minutes per run; the unit test covers all three branches in milliseconds and runs in CI on every PR.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A ŌĆö the file is new with this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A ŌĆö three new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New backend id: `claude-cli-interactive`. Selectable via `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` user config; nothing changes for users on `claude-cli`.
- When the new backend is active, every claude turn spawns a fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` process that boots a loopback MITM proxy on `127.0.0.1:<port>` for the lifetime of that turn. Process is killed on `end_turn`; loopback ports are released.
- Reasoning stream now carries plaintext `thinking_delta` content on the subscription path (previously visible only on the API-key billing path).
- Title-generation auxiliary turns (claude's side request whose only text content is `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}`) are buffered and silently suppressed before the wrapper emits any records to OpenClaw.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
OpenClaw gateway
  Ōöé  spawns one fresh wrapper per turn:
  Ō¢╝
bun wrapper.ts [--session-id ŌĆ”] [--resume ŌĆ”] [prompt-as-positional-arg]
  Ōö£ŌöĆ cert-manager.ts ŌåÆ generate/cache local CA + api.anthropic.com leaf cert
  Ōö£ŌöĆ mitm-server.ts  ŌåÆ Stage 1: HTTP CONNECT proxy (loopback only)
  Ōöé                    Stage 2: Bun TLS server (terminates TLS, re-issues upstream)
  Ōöé                    onEvent(evt) tap on every parsed SSE event
  Ō¢╝ NODE_OPTIONS="--require <abs>/tty-spoof.cjs"
    HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>
    NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=<ca.crt>   ŌåÉ scoped to this claude child only
  Ō¢╝
claude.exe (interactive mode, no -p, ink-based TUI rendering)
  Ōöé  API SSE ŌåÆ CONNECT ŌåÆ TLS terminate ŌåÆ tap event ŌåÆ forward to claude
  Ōöé  TUI stdout ŌåÆ /dev/null (we ignore it)
  Ō¢╝
wrapper.ts emits init / stream_event* / result JSONL on its own stdout,
sourced entirely from the tapped SSE ŌĆö output identical to the shape
`claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces.
```

### Per-turn kill/restart lifecycle (why we don't use the persistent live-session model)

`claude-cli-interactive` does **not** use the persistent `claude-stdio` live-session model that `claude-cli` uses. Each turn = one fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` invocation:

1. Wrapper boots, lazily generates/loads the local CA, starts the MITM proxy on a loopback port.
2. Wrapper emits `{"type":"init","session_id":"ŌĆ”"}` on its stdout.
3. Wrapper spawns claude over a piped stdin; framework stdin is forwarded to `claude.stdin` so OpenClaw's prompt-as-stdin convention still works.
4. SSE events flow through the proxy ŌåÆ wrapper's `onEvent` handler ŌåÆ wrapper emits `{"type":"stream_event","event":ŌĆ”}` JSONL per event.
5. On `message_stop` with `stop_reason === "end_turn"`, wrapper assembles `{"type":"result","session_id":"ŌĆ”","result":"ŌĆ”","is_error":false,"usage":{ŌĆ”}}`, drains it to stdout, sets `intentionalKill = true`, then `claude.kill()`s the child.
6. Wrapper exits 0; loopback ports released.

Why kill on `end_turn` instead of letting claude self-exit: interactive claude doesn't exit after one turn ŌĆö it's a TUI awaiting further input. The wrapper has to be authoritative about turn boundaries from the SSE side. `intentionalKill` distinguishes this from claude crashing.

### TTY spoof (`interactive-proxy/tty-spoof.cjs`)

Claude refuses interactive (subscription) mode unless stdin/stdout look like a TTY: it falls back to headless mode the moment `process.stdin.isTTY` is undefined or `tty.isatty(0)` returns false. Under OpenClaw it's spawned with piped stdio, so neither is true. `tty-spoof.cjs` is preloaded into the claude child via `NODE_OPTIONS=--require <abs-path>` and patches:

- `process.stdin.isTTY`, `process.stdout.isTTY`, `process.stderr.isTTY` ŌåÆ `true` (property level).
- `tty.isatty(fd)` ŌåÆ returns `true` for fd 0/1/2 (the ink renderer calls this directly via libuv, bypassing the property).
- `process.stdin.setRawMode` ŌåÆ no-op stub (ink calls this; would throw on a pipe).
- `process.stdout.columns`/`rows` and `process.stderr.columns`/`rows` ŌåÆ defaulted to 200├Ś50 so ink layout has dimensions to render against.

Minimum surface needed for claude to enter and stay in interactive mode under piped stdio. Nothing else is patched ŌĆö claude still does its real work, makes real API calls, and emits its real TUI bytes (we discard them).

### Full JSONL reconstruction (backwards compatibility with `claude -p --output-format stream-json`)

OpenClaw's JSONL parser expects exactly the output shape of `claude -p --output-format stream-json`. The wrapper reproduces it record-for-record, sourced entirely from the proxy:

- **`init`** (session_id) ŌĆö emitted on wrapper boot, before claude even spawns.
- **`stream_event`** (one per tapped SSE event) ŌĆö forwarded verbatim from the proxy: `message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta` (`text_delta` / `thinking_delta` / `signature_delta` / `tool_use`), `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`, `ping`. The proxy emits everything; the wrapper emits everything; OpenClaw's JSONL parser sees the same shape it would have on the `-p` path.
- **`result`** (one per turn) ŌĆö synthesised on `end_turn` from accumulated text-delta concatenation + final usage from `message_delta`.

The proxy is a thin pass-through ŌĆö it tees every parsed SSE event to a single `onEvent` handler and otherwise forwards the SSE stream unmodified to claude. No event-specific logic lives in the proxy; the wrapper interprets everything. To avoid wasted CPU on auxiliary turns the proxy only taps SSE responses for requests that contain a non-empty `tools` array (real conversation turns); title-gen and other no-tool requests pass through without inspection.

### Binary resolution

`normalizeConfig` runs after `mergeBackendConfig` shallow-spreads the inherited `claude-cli` override on top of this backend's base config. The merge replaces `command` with the inherited absolute claude binary path. The normalize step:

- Forces `command` back to `"bun"` so the wrapper actually runs as a script (otherwise `claude.exe` would be spawned with `wrapper.ts` as a positional and the proxy would never start).
- Stashes the inherited binary path on `env.OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` so `wrapper.ts` spawns that exact binary instead of doing a PATH lookup. PATH lookups on Windows resolve via PATHEXT to whichever `claude.exe` arrived first ŌĆö a winget install can shadow the npm install OpenClaw configured.
- **Guards the no-override case**: if `merged.command` is still our backend's own `"bun"` (no `claude-cli` override took effect), the env passthrough is **omitted** so `wrapper.ts` falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"` rather than trying to spawn bun as the claude binary.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **Yes** ŌĆö a per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child only via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store is not modified. Local CA + leaf private key are generated lazily on first use and cached under the user's OpenClaw config dir. The CA exists solely to issue a single `api.anthropic.com` leaf cert that the per-turn child trusts via the env var; no other process consumes it.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** ŌĆö the wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token. It is forwarded transparently in the request that the proxy re-issues upstream over real TLS.
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** ŌĆö claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. The proxy CONNECTs to the real upstream and reissues each request unmodified; the request body is read once to detect the `tools` field for the SSE-tap decision and is forwarded byte-for-byte. Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** ŌĆö same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools set (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same `--setting-sources user`, same `--session-id` semantics.
- Data access scope changed? **No** ŌĆö the proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing is exfiltrated, persisted, or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

Risk + mitigation summary for the Yes items: the trust boundary changes for Anthropic traffic of *this child process only*. Mitigation: child-scoped `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, no system CA mutation, CA private key restricted to the user's OpenClaw config dir, no other process configured to trust it. Backend is opt-in by id (`claude-cli-interactive`), not a default. The whole reason for this surface is to keep OpenClaw on the interactive subscription pool after the June 15 split ŌĆö the `-p` (programmatic) path that doesn't intercept TLS remains the default, and users keep that experience by selecting the existing `claude-cli` backend.

## Prior art / alternatives considered

- [`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) takes a different approach to keeping Claude on the interactive subscription pool: it runs `claude` without `-p` and bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely. That works for the raw final text, but **loses the JSONL `stream_event` records that any reasoning stream or tool-use rendering depends on**. The JSONL-reconstruction approach in this PR (re-emitting the same record shape `claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces, sourced from the SSE tap) preserves the existing OpenClaw stream consumer unchanged ŌĆö so the existing reasoning bridge and the tool-use surface in the follow-up PR layer on cleanly without losing fidelity.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** ŌĆö the existing `claude-cli` backend is unchanged. Adding the new backend is opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- Config/env changes? **No** ŌĆö no new required user config. New optional env var `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stderr from the wrapper.
- Migration needed? **No**.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a newly-installed `claude-cli` user with no inherited override could end up spawning `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` blindly stores `merged.command` in the env passthrough.
  - Mitigation: `normalizeConfig` skips the env passthrough when `merged.command === "bun"`; the wrapper falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"`. Regression test in `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` asserts the env var is `undefined` in that branch.
- Risk: the `tty-spoof.cjs` preload is a runtime support file, not a build output, so it could be omitted from packaged installs and break interactive mode at runtime with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.
  - Mitigation: the file is declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets` so the postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper, mitm-server, and cert-manager.
- Risk: a winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install on Windows because `spawn("claude")` resolves via PATHEXT, leading to `unknown option '--effort'` on older claude versions.
  - Mitigation: when an override is present, `wrapper.ts` spawns the exact binary the parent backend resolved (passed via `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY`) instead of falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- Risk: title-generation side requests can land mid-turn and inject `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - Mitigation: the wrapper buffers early `text_delta`s when the accumulated text starts with `{`; if it parses as JSON with a `title` field the entire turn is suppressed before any record is emitted to OpenClaw. Defence-in-depth: the proxy already skips SSE tapping for requests without a `tools` array, so most auxiliary requests never reach the wrapper at all.
- Risk: TLS interception persists a local CA + leaf key under the user's OpenClaw config dir indefinitely; an attacker with read access to that directory could craft a leaf cert trusted by the per-turn child.
  - Mitigation: trust is scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` (no other process opts in), the system trust store is unchanged, and the CA exists solely to issue the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs. Maintainer security review explicitly invited as part of this PR.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend ŌĆö stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Motivation (dated, citable).** On **May 13, 2026** Anthropic announced that, starting **June 15, 2026**, Claude subscriptions split into two pools: **interactive usage stays on existing subscription limits** (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai), while **programmatic usage moves onto a separate fixed monthly credit pool** ŌĆö explicitly enumerated as Agent SDK, `claude -p` (the non-interactive command), Claude Code GitHub Actions, **and third-party apps that auth with the subscription through the Agent SDK** (i.e. OpenClaw on the existing `claude-cli` backend). The programmatic credit is fixed per tier (Pro $20 / Max 5x $100 / Max 20x $200 / Team $100/seat / Enterprise $200/seat), billed at API rates, and does **not** roll over. Coverage: [VentureBeat ŌĆö "Anthropic reinstates OpenClaw and third-party agent usage on Claude subscriptions ŌĆö with a catch"](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch), [SiliconANGLE ŌĆö "Anthropic announces ŌĆśprogrammatic credit poolŌĆÖ as agentic tool use rises"](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/14/anthropic-announces-programmatic-credit-pool-agentic-tool-use-rises/).
- **Why this PR exists.** OpenClaw's existing `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` ŌĆö i.e. exactly the non-interactive command the announcement names as moving to the programmatic credit pool on June 15. After June 15 every OpenClaw turn on a Claude subscription will drain the fixed credit pool at API rates rather than count against the user's interactive subscription limits, and Opus turns in particular will exhaust the credit very quickly. This PR adds a second backend that drives `claude` in interactive mode (no `-p`) so OpenClaw stays on the interactive pool that subscribers are actually paying for.
- **What changed.** A new `claude-cli-interactive` backend runs `claude` without `-p` (interactive mode) under a per-turn `bun` wrapper that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy, taps every API SSE event to reconstruct `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records on its own stdout, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload tricks claude into staying in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** The existing `claude-cli` backend is untouched (its predicate `liveSession === "claude-stdio"` matches it alone now); user `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` overrides flow through to the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with only `-p`-only flags stripped; the system CA store is **not** mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` is scoped to the spawned claude child only); non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) pass through unmodified; tool-use / timestamp / "waiting on tool" injection into the reasoning stream is intentionally **out of scope** here and lands as a separate follow-up to #81550 that rides on the `stream_event` JSONL surface this PR establishes.
- AI-assisted: yes ŌĆö drafted with Claude.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge ŌĆö this PR introduces the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend; the follow-up tool-use injection PR will work on both)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression
- [x] This PR adds a new backend behind an opt-in backend id

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `claude` running under OpenClaw in **interactive (subscription) mode** with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- Real environment tested: Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm install), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  - `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌĆö focused vitest run of the anthropic extension test files.
  - Set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` as the active backend in user config, restart the OpenClaw gateway, send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn, observe the wrapper boot in stderr, the API SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>` and arrive at OpenClaw as `stream_event` records.
  - Send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` (default sessionMode) and verify the second turn picks up the existing claude session.
  - With `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` set, verify stderr includes `[openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: ŌĆ”\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` (the npm-installed claude, not a winget-shadowed install).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm test:extension anthropic` ŌåÆ `Test Files 7 passed (7) / Tests 73 passed (73) / Duration 10.41s`. New `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` covers the three normalizeConfig branches (override path ŌåÆ `command="bun"` + env passthrough; preserves other env entries; **no override ŌåÆ env passthrough omitted** so wrapper.ts can fall back to a PATH lookup of `claude`).
  - Live Telegram turn: per-turn process emits `init` ŌåÆ `stream_event*` ŌåÆ single `result` on `end_turn`, all sourced from the proxy's SSE tap.
- Observed result after fix: per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly ŌĆö wrapper emits `init`, then a continuous stream of `stream_event` records (tapped from the proxy), then a single `result` record on `end_turn`, then sets `intentionalKill = true` and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits with code 0.
- **Live capture from the running install** ŌĆö redacted artifacts on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch of my fork:
  - [`.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt) ŌĆö 30 lines from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` covering three real per-turn invocations: gateway logs `cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive` + `cli argv: bun <NPM>\ŌĆ”\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose ŌĆ”`, wrapper stderr logs `certs ready` ŌåÆ `MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081` ŌåÆ `spawning claude binary: <NPM>\ŌĆ”\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` ŌåÆ `result flushed, waiting for session write before kill` ŌåÆ `session file updated, killing claude`. Verbatim, just paths/session-ids redacted.
  - [`.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt) ŌĆö `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` directory listing + `openssl x509 -noout` output for both certs. CA: `CN=OpenClaw Proxy CA`, self-signed, valid `May 14 22:30:14 2026 GMT` ŌåÆ `May 14 22:30:14 2027 GMT`, SHA1 `63:69:13:08:D9:AE:CC:CC:85:D3:A7:B2:AB:E0:A6:06:E3:88:97:2E`. Leaf: `CN=api.anthropic.com`, issued by the CA, SAN `DNS:api.anthropic.com, DNS:statsig.anthropic.com, DNS:console.anthropic.com`.
  - [`.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json) ŌĆö `agents.defaults.model.primary` is `claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-6`; `agents.defaults.models` registers `claude-cli-interactive` runtimes for opus-4-7/4-6/4-5, sonnet-4-6/4-5, haiku-4-5. Production wiring, not a test fixture.

  Embedded sample (one of the three turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

  ```
  cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus promptChars=N trigger=user useResume=true session=present resumeSession=<SESSION> reuse=reusable historyPrompt=present
  cli argv: bun <NPM>\node_modules\openclaw\dist\extensions\anthropic\interactive-proxy\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --permission-mode bypassPermissions --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <TMP>\openclaw-cli-mcp-<TMP>\mcp.json --model opus <prompt:N chars>
  [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
  [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 -- Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
  [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
  [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill, path: <HOME>\.claude\projects\<WORKSPACE>\<SESSION>.jsonl
  [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
  [openclaw-proxy] claude exited, code:  signal: SIGTERM intentional: true
  ```
- What was not tested:
  - Linux/macOS (developed and exercised on Windows 11 only); the wrapper has no Windows-specific code beyond the binary-resolution guard, but I have not run it on Linux/macOS.
  - Bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only the `mcp__openclaw__*` allowlist path was exercised live).
  - Behaviour against an Anthropic-side `429`/`overloaded_error` mid-stream (the wrapper passes the SSE stream through unchanged, so the existing claude error-handling should apply, but I did not force the failure to confirm).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö this is a feature PR, not a bugfix. The condition this addresses (no thinking visibility on subscription) is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` ŌĆö asserts `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape (forces `command="bun"`, stashes inherited binary on env when an override exists, omits the env when no override exists).
- Scenario the test should lock in: the post-merge normalize step survives both shapes the runtime can produce ŌĆö (a) user has a `claude-cli` override ŌåÆ `merged.command` is the inherited absolute path, env passthrough must be set; (b) user has no override ŌåÆ `merged.command` stays as the backend's own `"bun"`, env passthrough must be **omitted** so wrapper.ts doesn't try to spawn bun as the claude binary.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig`, fully exercised by calling `backend.normalizeConfig(merged)` synchronously and asserting the output keys. An e2e run would also catch it but cost minutes per run; the unit test covers all three branches in milliseconds and runs in CI on every PR.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A ŌĆö the file is new with this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A ŌĆö three new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New backend id: `claude-cli-interactive`. Selectable via `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` user config; nothing changes for users on `claude-cli`.
- When the new backend is active, every claude turn spawns a fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` process that boots a loopback MITM proxy on `127.0.0.1:<port>` for the lifetime of that turn. Process is killed on `end_turn`; loopback ports are released.
- Reasoning stream now carries plaintext `thinking_delta` content on the subscription path (previously visible only on the API-key billing path).
- Title-generation auxiliary turns (claude's side request whose only text content is `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}`) are buffered and silently suppressed before the wrapper emits any records to OpenClaw.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
OpenClaw gateway
  Ōöé  spawns one fresh wrapper per turn:
  Ō¢╝
bun wrapper.ts [--session-id ŌĆ”] [--resume ŌĆ”] [prompt-as-positional-arg]
  Ōö£ŌöĆ cert-manager.ts ŌåÆ generate/cache local CA + api.anthropic.com leaf cert
  Ōö£ŌöĆ mitm-server.ts  ŌåÆ Stage 1: HTTP CONNECT proxy (loopback only)
  Ōöé                    Stage 2: Bun TLS server (terminates TLS, re-issues upstream)
  Ōöé                    onEvent(evt) tap on every parsed SSE event
  Ō¢╝ NODE_OPTIONS="--require <abs>/tty-spoof.cjs"
    HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>
    NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=<ca.crt>   ŌåÉ scoped to this claude child only
  Ō¢╝
claude.exe (interactive mode, no -p, ink-based TUI rendering)
  Ōöé  API SSE ŌåÆ CONNECT ŌåÆ TLS terminate ŌåÆ tap event ŌåÆ forward to claude
  Ōöé  TUI stdout ŌåÆ /dev/null (we ignore it)
  Ō¢╝
wrapper.ts emits init / stream_event* / result JSONL on its own stdout,
sourced entirely from the tapped SSE ŌĆö output identical to the shape
`claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces.
```

### Per-turn kill/restart lifecycle (why we don't use the persistent live-session model)

`claude-cli-interactive` does **not** use the persistent `claude-stdio` live-session model that `claude-cli` uses. Each turn = one fresh `bun wrapper.ts ŌĆ”` invocation:

1. Wrapper boots, lazily generates/loads the local CA, starts the MITM proxy on a loopback port.
2. Wrapper emits `{"type":"init","session_id":"ŌĆ”"}` on its stdout.
3. Wrapper spawns claude over a piped stdin; framework stdin is forwarded to `claude.stdin` so OpenClaw's prompt-as-stdin convention still works.
4. SSE events flow through the proxy ŌåÆ wrapper's `onEvent` handler ŌåÆ wrapper emits `{"type":"stream_event","event":ŌĆ”}` JSONL per event.
5. On `message_stop` with `stop_reason === "end_turn"`, wrapper assembles `{"type":"result","session_id":"ŌĆ”","result":"ŌĆ”","is_error":false,"usage":{ŌĆ”}}`, drains it to stdout, sets `intentionalKill = true`, then `claude.kill()`s the child.
6. Wrapper exits 0; loopback ports released.

Why kill on `end_turn` instead of letting claude self-exit: interactive claude doesn't exit after one turn ŌĆö it's a TUI awaiting further input. The wrapper has to be authoritative about turn boundaries from the SSE side. `intentionalKill` distinguishes this from claude crashing.

### TTY spoof (`interactive-proxy/tty-spoof.cjs`)

Claude refuses interactive (subscription) mode unless stdin/stdout look like a TTY: it falls back to headless mode the moment `process.stdin.isTTY` is undefined or `tty.isatty(0)` returns false. Under OpenClaw it's spawned with piped stdio, so neither is true. `tty-spoof.cjs` is preloaded into the claude child via `NODE_OPTIONS=--require <abs-path>` and patches:

- `process.stdin.isTTY`, `process.stdout.isTTY`, `process.stderr.isTTY` ŌåÆ `true` (property level).
- `tty.isatty(fd)` ŌåÆ returns `true` for fd 0/1/2 (the ink renderer calls this directly via libuv, bypassing the property).
- `process.stdin.setRawMode` ŌåÆ no-op stub (ink calls this; would throw on a pipe).
- `process.stdout.columns`/`rows` and `process.stderr.columns`/`rows` ŌåÆ defaulted to 200├Ś50 so ink layout has dimensions to render against.

Minimum surface needed for claude to enter and stay in interactive mode under piped stdio. Nothing else is patched ŌĆö claude still does its real work, makes real API calls, and emits its real TUI bytes (we discard them).

### Full JSONL reconstruction (backwards compatibility with `claude -p --output-format stream-json`)

OpenClaw's JSONL parser expects exactly the output shape of `claude -p --output-format stream-json`. The wrapper reproduces it record-for-record, sourced entirely from the proxy:

- **`init`** (session_id) ŌĆö emitted on wrapper boot, before claude even spawns.
- **`stream_event`** (one per tapped SSE event) ŌĆö forwarded verbatim from the proxy: `message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta` (`text_delta` / `thinking_delta` / `signature_delta` / `tool_use`), `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`, `ping`. The proxy emits everything; the wrapper emits everything; OpenClaw's JSONL parser sees the same shape it would have on the `-p` path.
- **`result`** (one per turn) ŌĆö synthesised on `end_turn` from accumulated text-delta concatenation + final usage from `message_delta`.

The proxy is a thin pass-through ŌĆö it tees every parsed SSE event to a single `onEvent` handler and otherwise forwards the SSE stream unmodified to claude. No event-specific logic lives in the proxy; the wrapper interprets everything. To avoid wasted CPU on auxiliary turns the proxy only taps SSE responses for requests that contain a non-empty `tools` array (real conversation turns); title-gen and other no-tool requests pass through without inspection.

### Binary resolution

`normalizeConfig` runs after `mergeBackendConfig` shallow-spreads the inherited `claude-cli` override on top of this backend's base config. The merge replaces `command` with the inherited absolute claude binary path. The normalize step:

- Forces `command` back to `"bun"` so the wrapper actually runs as a script (otherwise `claude.exe` would be spawned with `wrapper.ts` as a positional and the proxy would never start).
- Stashes the inherited binary path on `env.OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` so `wrapper.ts` spawns that exact binary instead of doing a PATH lookup. PATH lookups on Windows resolve via PATHEXT to whichever `claude.exe` arrived first ŌĆö a winget install can shadow the npm install OpenClaw configured.
- **Guards the no-override case**: if `merged.command` is still our backend's own `"bun"` (no `claude-cli` override took effect), the env passthrough is **omitted** so `wrapper.ts` falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"` rather than trying to spawn bun as the claude binary.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **Yes** ŌĆö a per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child only via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store is not modified. Local CA + leaf private key are generated lazily on first use and cached under the user's OpenClaw config dir. The CA exists solely to issue a single `api.anthropic.com` leaf cert that the per-turn child trusts via the env var; no other process consumes it.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** ŌĆö the wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token. It is forwarded transparently in the request that the proxy re-issues upstream over real TLS.
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** ŌĆö claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. The proxy CONNECTs to the real upstream and reissues each request unmodified; the request body is read once to detect the `tools` field for the SSE-tap decision and is forwarded byte-for-byte. Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** ŌĆö same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools set (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same `--setting-sources user`, same `--session-id` semantics.
- Data access scope changed? **No** ŌĆö the proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing is exfiltrated, persisted, or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

Risk + mitigation summary for the Yes items: the trust boundary changes for Anthropic traffic of *this child process only*. Mitigation: child-scoped `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, no system CA mutation, CA private key restricted to the user's OpenClaw config dir, no other process configured to trust it. Backend is opt-in by id (`claude-cli-interactive`), not a default. The whole reason for this surface is to keep OpenClaw on the interactive subscription pool after the June 15 split ŌĆö the `-p` (programmatic) path that doesn't intercept TLS remains the default, and users keep that experience by selecting the existing `claude-cli` backend.

## Prior art / alternatives considered

- [`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) takes a different approach to keeping Claude on the interactive subscription pool: it runs `claude` without `-p` and bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely. That works for the raw final text, but **loses the JSONL `stream_event` records that any reasoning stream or tool-use rendering depends on**. The JSONL-reconstruction approach in this PR (re-emitting the same record shape `claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces, sourced from the SSE tap) preserves the existing OpenClaw stream consumer unchanged ŌĆö so the existing reasoning bridge and the tool-use surface in the follow-up PR layer on cleanly without losing fidelity.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** ŌĆö the existing `claude-cli` backend is unchanged. Adding the new backend is opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- Config/env changes? **No** ŌĆö no new required user config. New optional env var `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stderr from the wrapper.
- Migration needed? **No**.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a newly-installed `claude-cli` user with no inherited override could end up spawning `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` blindly stores `merged.command` in the env passthrough.
  - Mitigation: `normalizeConfig` skips the env passthrough when `merged.command === "bun"`; the wrapper falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"`. Regression test in `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` asserts the env var is `undefined` in that branch.
- Risk: the `tty-spoof.cjs` preload is a runtime support file, not a build output, so it could be omitted from packaged installs and break interactive mode at runtime with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.
  - Mitigation: the file is declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets` so the postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper, mitm-server, and cert-manager.
- Risk: a winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install on Windows because `spawn("claude")` resolves via PATHEXT, leading to `unknown option '--effort'` on older claude versions.
  - Mitigation: when an override is present, `wrapper.ts` spawns the exact binary the parent backend resolved (passed via `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY`) instead of falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- Risk: title-generation side requests can land mid-turn and inject `{"title":"ŌĆ”"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - Mitigation: the wrapper buffers early `text_delta`s when the accumulated text starts with `{`; if it parses as JSON with a `title` field the entire turn is suppressed before any record is emitted to OpenClaw. Defence-in-depth: the proxy already skips SSE tapping for requests without a `tools` array, so most auxiliary requests never reach the wrapper at all.
- Risk: TLS interception persists a local CA + leaf key under the user's OpenClaw config dir indefinitely; an attacker with read access to that directory could craft a leaf cert trusted by the per-turn child.
  - Mitigation: trust is scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` (no other process opts in), the system trust store is unchanged, and the CA exists solely to issue the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs. Maintainer security review explicitly invited as part of this PR.
